title: rubus niveus subsp. leucocarpus
intro: Rubus niveus subsp. leucocarpus, also known as the white-fruited Himalayan raspberry or white Himalayan raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus niveus plant, which is a type of raspberry native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to soothe digestive issues and reduce oxidative stress. The health benefits of Rubus niveus subsp. leucocarpus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support immune function, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application or internal use.
